items.scenario: You recently started a summer internship at a marketing firm, and your supervisor asked you to shadow client calls this week. You are unsure how to prepare and want some guidance before the first call on Thursday morning.
items.direction: Write an email to Ms. Bennett. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank her for the chance to join the client calls
Ask what you should review beforehand
Explain your background with client communication
items.subject: Preparing for Thursday's client calls

items.scenario: You volunteer at a neighborhood community garden that recently switched to a new online sign-up system for weekend shifts. The system keeps double-booking people, and last Saturday four volunteers all showed up to weed the same plot.
items.direction: Write an email to Mr. Alvarez. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with the new sign-up system
Explain how the double-booking affected last Saturday
Suggest a way to fix the scheduling
items.subject: Scheduling mix-ups in the volunteer sign-up system

items.scenario: You moved into a student apartment two weeks ago, and the hot water in your unit shuts off every evening around seven. Your roommate has the same trouble, and the building's laundry room has stayed locked since Monday.
items.direction: Write an email to Ms. Foster. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the hot water and laundry room problems
Ask when repairs can be scheduled
items.subject: Hot water and laundry issues in my unit

items.scenario: You ordered a desk lamp online for next-day delivery, but it arrived four days late with a cracked base. The company charged you an express shipping fee, and the return page on their site will not load at all.
items.direction: Write an email to Mr. Reyes. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problems with the lamp and the delivery
Provide the details of your original order
Ask for a refund of the express shipping fee
items.subject: Late, damaged lamp and a broken return page
